zh.lifeSummary: 當Constance de Lyham於大約1299年出生在英國諾圖波藍德哈頓時，她的父親William De Lyham25歲，母親Mary21歲。她於大約1320年與Alan de Strother在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Constance de Lyham于大约1299年出生在英国諾圖波藍德哈頓时，她的父亲William De Lyham25岁，母亲Mary21岁。她于大约1320年与Alan de Strother在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Lyham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic Riad, Samir, Abir, Amin, Hala, Jamil, Nadim, Soad, Walid, Wedad.
Arabic (mainly from Lebanon and Syria): occupational name from laḥḥām ‘butcher’. This surname is found mostly among Christians.

nameMeanings.name: Constance
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval form of the Late Latin name Constantia, which is either a feminine derivative of Constans ( see Constant ) or an abstract noun meaning ‘constancy’. This was a popular name among the Normans, and was borne by, amongst others, the formidable Constance of Sicily ( 1158–98 ), wife of the Emperor Henry VI .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Constance de Lyham was born about 1299, in Chatton, Northumberland, England, her father, William De Lyham, was 25 and her mother, Mary, was 21. She married Alan de Strother about 1320, in Northumberland, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Constance de Lyham est née environ 1299, à Chatton, Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, William De Lyham, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mary, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Alan de Strother environ 1320, à Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Constance de Lyham um 1299 in Chatton, Northumberland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William De Lyham 25 und ihre Mutter Mary 21. Sie hat um 1320 in Northumberland, England, United Kingdom Alan de Strother geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Constance de Lyham è nata il circa 1299 nel Chatton, Northumberland, England, sua padre, William De Lyham, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Mary, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Alan de Strother circa 1320, nel Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Constance de Lyhamが約1299年にイギリスノースウンバーランドチャットオンで生まれた時，父William De Lyhamは25歳，母Maryは21歳であった。約1320年にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドでAlan de Strotherと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Constance de Lyham(이)가 대략 1299년 영국 노르스벌랜드 채턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William De Lyham(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 대략 1320년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드에서 Alan de Strother(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Constance de Lyham nasceu aproximadamente 1299, em Chatton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, William De Lyham, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mary, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Alan de Strother aproximadamente 1320, em Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Constance de Lyham родилась около 1299 г. в местности чаттон, нортумбирланд, Англия, её отцу по имени William De Lyham было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Alan de Strother около 1320 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Constance de Lyham nació aproximadamente 1299, en Chatton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, William De Lyham, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mary, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Alan de Strother aproximadamente 1320, en Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
